profile.name: David Vilela
profile.headline: PhD Engineer · AI, Blockchain & Distributed Systems
profile.tagline: From physics simulations to decentralized autonomous agents — bridging research and real-world software
bio: I am a passionate technologist with a strong focus on Multi-Agent Systems, Distributed Ledger Technologies, and Machine Learning. With nearly 15 years of experience ranging from advanced R&D to fast-paced startups, I bridge the gap between academic rigor and practical software engineering.

My career has evolved from solving complex physics simulations to building decentralized AI autonomous systems. I co-authored the Open Autonomy and Open AEA frameworks, and designed autonomous agents used across the Olas ecosystem.

My research background includes a PhD in Engineering, 5 published papers (44 citations) in journals like Multibody System Dynamics, a research stay at the University of Bremen, and an international benchmarking platform for the multibody dynamics community.

A natural communicator and mentor, I co-organize CedeCiencia — a volunteer-led science lecture series in Galicia — and share knowledge through developer relations, university teaching, and my YouTube channel on science and technology.
profile.footer_text: Open to interesting conversations about AI, distributed systems, and open source.

publications.title: Releasing iwa: a Python framework for multi-chain crypto apps
publications.summary: First alpha of iwa — 34,000 lines of code, built as a 2-month experiment testing AI coding agents (Claude, Gemini, Antigravity).

publications.title: CedeCiencia: bringing science talks to Cedeira
publications.summary: Continuing the organization of CedeCiencia, an initiative bringing science and culture lectures to the community.

publications.title: Vera: an AI agent fighting election misinformation
publications.summary: Introducing Vera, an autonomous agent on the Olas network that tracks fake news, monitors social media, and generates fact-checked responses.

publications.title: Versos, cantigas e bits — AI-generated Galician music
publications.summary: An experimental music collection blending classic Galician poetry with AI-generated electronic production, available on Spotify and Apple Music.

publications.title: Speaking at Pint of Science: 'Ponerle el cascabel a la IA'
publications.summary: Talk in Ferrol about the risks of centralized AI and why decentralization matters.

publications.title: Tsunami: an autonomous Olas ecosystem tracker
publications.summary: An agent that monitors the Olas blockchain and reports on Twitter/Farcaster using a local Llama model with random AI personalities.

publications.title: The case for Blockchain and Autonomy
publications.summary: How blockchain and autonomous agents create decentralized services without central authorities — published on the Olas Network blog.

speaking.title: Olas Dev Academy

speaking.title: CedeCiencia
speaking.description: Co-organizer of a volunteer-led annual science and culture lecture series in the bars of Cedeira, Galicia. Speaker on AI risks and decentralization.

speaking.title: Pint of Science
speaking.description: Speaker at Pint of Science 2024 in Ferrol: 'Ponerle el cascabel a la IA' — on the risks of centralized AI.

speaking.title: Hackathon Mentoring
speaking.description: Mentor and judge at ETH and Web3 hackathons, supporting the Olas developer ecosystem.

speaking.title: University Lecturer
speaking.description: Taught CAD and SolidWorks courses at the University of A Coruña during PhD.

speaking.title: YouTube Science Channel
speaking.description: Educational content on science, technology and programming — simulations, AI, and hardware projects.

experience.title: Independent Engineer — Sabbatical
experience.description: Building personal projects and exploring AI-assisted software development. Currently developing iwa, a Python framework for multi-chain crypto apps and AI agent tooling, and indie games (Siege and Birus).

experience.title: Senior Python Engineer / Agent Engineer
experience.subtitle: Valory AG
experience.description: Founding member of the Olas protocol. Contributed to the core of the Open Autonomy and Open AEA frameworks. Designed and implemented decentralized AI agents and economies like Contribute, Governatoooorr, and Agents.fun, and contributed to Mechs and Predict. Team Lead of the Agents.fun team, driving architecture decisions and coordinating task planning and distribution across developers. Led the Olas Dev Academy, teaching developers to build decentralized AI agents. Acted as Dev Rel, mentoring at hackathons and supporting the ecosystem.

experience.title: Machine Learning Platform Engineer
experience.subtitle: CTAG, Automobile Technological Center of Galicia
experience.description: Architected and implemented a high-throughput sensor data platform for autonomous vehicles. Engineered solutions for recording, synchronizing, and decoding massive streams of sensor data to fuel machine learning models. Spearheaded the adoption of DevOps practices, streamlining workflows with Docker and CI/CD pipelines.

experience.title: Research Support Technician
experience.subtitle: Mechanical Engineering Laboratory, University of A Coruña
experience.description: Conducted advanced research on volumetric force models based on sphere discretizations. Validated theoretical models through rigorous experimentation and authored two research papers presenting novel findings in computer graphics and physics simulation.

experience.title: Predoctoral Researcher and PhD Teaching Assistant
experience.subtitle: Mechanical Engineering Laboratory, University of A Coruña
experience.description: Engineered a real-time multibody simulator featuring precise collision detection and volumetric force modeling for conforming contacts. Designed and built a VR haptic feedback glove from the ground up, including electronics and control software. Taught CAD and SolidWorks at the university level. Collaborated with the Computer Graphics Group at the University of Bremen.

experience.title: Research Assistant
experience.subtitle: Mechanical Engineering Laboratory, University of A Coruña
experience.description: Developed a real-time multibody vehicle simulator for hardware-in-the-loop testing of vehicle dynamics. Created a benchmarking platform for multibody algorithms to facilitate international scientific collaboration and performance ranking.
